title: elatostema ranongense var. glabrum
intro: Elatostema ranongense var. glabrum, also known as Thai vine or Thai creeping fern, is a type of flowering plant in the Urticaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Thai medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Elatostema ranongense var. glabrum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can be consumed orally to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
